summary: Laid‑back, blues‑heavy Memphis show with strong performances (Take Me to the River, When I Paint My Masterpiece, Watchtower → Standing on the Moon) captured in an unknown‑source recording of fair quality.
review: The concert is praised for its relaxed vibe and memorable moments – notably the first‑ever Dead rendition of “Take Me to the River,” a powerful “When I Paint My Masterpiece,” and a seamless Watchtower → Standing on the Moon pairing that grew out of an inspired Space jam. Recording details are sparse, but listeners found the audio adequate enough to enjoy the performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is highlighted as good to great by one detailed review; recording quality is not discussed, leading to a moderate overall rating with medium confidence due to only two reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments on sound clarity or mix; reviewers focus solely on performance, implying the audio is at least listenable but not notably high‑fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed, blues‑inflected first set followed by a spacious second set where an exploratory Drums/Space segues into a well‑executed Watchtower → Standing on the Moon combo, creating a cohesive and memorable flow.

band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboardist) teases ‘The Wheel’ during Space, contributing to the improvisational peak.
